title: AccountabilitySignals
description: A bilingual, motivational signal emitted by Module 15 (Social) to the Inbox digest pipeline and to Habits (umbrella Provides list). Signals are NOT money actions and NOT standalone push — Inbox owns notification budget/restraint (ux-foundations §6, SC-009). A signal MUST NOT carry a raw monetary amount, account identifier, or institution name. Any 'contribute to your goal' nudge is evaluated DOWNSTREAM against Cash Safety precedence — Social never overrides a safety signal (umbrella precedence rule).

signal_id.description: Stable id; idempotency/dedup key for the Inbox pipeline (same circle_id + signal_type + subject within 24h = one item, ux-foundations §6.3).

circle_id.description: Originating circle. Recipient must be an authorized member; authZ enforced server-side (Threat Model, SC-015).

member_id.description: Opaque circle-scoped member id of the recipient — not an account/institution identifier.

signal_type.description: The motivational event. None of these carry a money amount.

priority_tier.description: Maps to ux-foundations §6.1 notification hierarchy. Social signals are at most 'important' (digest push, bundled) — never 'critical' (Social has no time-sensitive safety event). Inbox enforces the tier and the per-user notification budget.

payload.description: Bilingual short description + deep link, per ux-foundations §6.3. MUST NOT embed a monetary amount, account, or institution.

short_description_en.description: EN short description (e.g. 'Maple hit a milestone in Summer trip').

action_url.description: Deep link into the Social circle view (opened via the Inbox tab, not a module-specific push).

expires_at.description: UTC expiry for time-bounded signals (e.g. streak_at_risk). Null for non-expiring informational signals.

freshness.description: Freshness of the underlying progress the signal is derived from; a stale source ⇒ the signal is suppressed/flagged rather than emitted on old data (FR-X-008).
